root_text.an10.119:1.1: Tena kho pana samayena jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo tadahuposathe sīsaṁnhāto navaṁ khomayugaṁ nivattho allakusamuṭṭhiṁ ādāya bhagavato avidūre ekamantaṁ ṭhito hoti.
root_text.an10.119:2.1: Addasā kho bhagavā jāṇussoṇiṁ brāhmaṇaṁ tadahuposathe sīsaṁnhātaṁ navaṁ khomayugaṁ nivatthaṁ allakusamuṭṭhiṁ ādāya ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ.
root_text.an10.119:2.3: “kiṁ nu tvaṁ, brāhmaṇa, tadahuposathe sīsaṁnhāto navaṁ khomayugaṁ nivattho allakusamuṭṭhiṁ ādāya ekamantaṁ ṭhito?
root_text.an10.119:3.1: “Yathā kathaṁ pana, brāhmaṇa, brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī hotī”ti?
root_text.an10.119:3.2: “Idha, bho gotama, brāhmaṇā tadahuposathe sīsaṁnhātā navaṁ khomayugaṁ nivatthā allena gomayena pathaviṁ opuñjitvā haritehi kusehi pattharitvā antarā ca velaṁ antarā ca agyāgāraṁ seyyaṁ kappenti.
root_text.an10.119:3.3: Te taṁ rattiṁ tikkhattuṁ paccuṭṭhāya pañjalikā aggiṁ namassanti:
root_text.an10.119:3.6: Tassā ca rattiyā accayena paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena brāhmaṇe santappenti.
root_text.an10.119:3.7: Evaṁ, bho gotama, brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī hotī”ti.
root_text.an10.119:4.1: “Aññathā kho, brāhmaṇa, brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī hoti, aññathā ca pana ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hotī”ti.
root_text.an10.119:4.2: “Yathā kathaṁ pana, bho gotama, ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hoti?
root_text.an10.119:4.3: Sādhu me bhavaṁ gotamo tathā dhammaṁ desetu yathā ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hotī”ti.
root_text.an10.119:5.1: “Tena hi, brāhmaṇa, suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an10.119:5.2: “Evaṁ, bho”ti kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an10.119:15.5: Evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hotī”ti.
root_text.an10.119:16.1: “Aññathā, bho gotama, brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī, aññathā ca pana ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hoti.
root_text.an10.119:16.2: Imissā ca, bho gotama, ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇiyā brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī kalaṁ nāgghati soḷasiṁ.
root_text.an10.119:16.4: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an10.119:1.1: Now, at that time it was the sabbath. The brahmin Jānussoṇi had bathed his head and dressed in a new pair of linen robes. Holding a handful of fresh grass, he stood to one side not far from the Buddha.
translation_text.an10.119:2.3: “Brahmin, why have you bathed your head and dressed in a new pair of linen robes? Why are you standing to one side holding a handful of fresh grass?
translation_text.an10.119:2.4: What’s going on today with the brahmin clan?”
translation_text.an10.119:2.5: “Worthy Gotama, today is the ceremony of descent for the brahmin clan.”
translation_text.an10.119:3.1: “But how do the brahmins observe the ceremony of descent?”
translation_text.an10.119:3.2: “Well, worthy Gotama, on the sabbath the brahmins bathe their heads and dress in a new pair of linen robes. They make a heap of fresh cow dung and spread it with green grass. Then they make their beds between the boundary and the fire chamber.
translation_text.an10.119:3.3: That night they rise three times and worship the fire with joined palms:
translation_text.an10.119:3.5: And they serve the fire with abundant ghee, oil, and butter.
translation_text.an10.119:3.6: And when the night has passed they serve the brahmins with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.an10.119:3.7: That’s how the brahmins observe the ceremony of descent.”
translation_text.an10.119:4.1: “The ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is quite different from that observed in the training of the Noble One.”
translation_text.an10.119:4.2: “But worthy Gotama, how is the ceremony of descent observed in the training of the Noble One?
translation_text.an10.119:4.3: Worthy Gotama, please teach me this.”
translation_text.an10.119:5.1: “Well then, brahmin, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an10.119:6.3: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:6.4: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong view,
translation_text.an10.119:7.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:7.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong thought,
translation_text.an10.119:8.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:8.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong speech,
translation_text.an10.119:9.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:9.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong action,
translation_text.an10.119:10.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:10.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong livelihood,
translation_text.an10.119:11.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:11.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong effort,
translation_text.an10.119:12.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:12.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong mindfulness,
translation_text.an10.119:13.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:13.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong immersion,
translation_text.an10.119:14.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:14.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong knowledge,
translation_text.an10.119:15.2: in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an10.119:15.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong freedom,
translation_text.an10.119:15.5: This is the ceremony of descent in the training of the Noble One.”
translation_text.an10.119:16.1: “The ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is quite different from that observed in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.an10.119:16.2: And, worthy Gotama, the ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is not worth a sixteenth part of a master of the ceremony of descent observed in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.an10.119:16.4: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.an10.119:1.1: jāṇussoṇi → jānussoni (si); jānussoṇi (si); jāṇusoṇi (mr) | sīsaṁnhāto → sīsaṁnahāto (bj, pts1ed); sīsanhāto (sya-all)
variant_text.an10.119:2.4: nvajja → kiṁ nu ajja (sya-all); kiṁ nu kho ajja (pts1ed); kiṁ nu khvajja (mr) | brāhmaṇakulassā”ti → brāhmaṇa brahmakusalassāti (mr)
